title: skills:skill
description: Defines the core domain models for skills: `Skill` and `SkillTemplate`. These are pure domain types with no I/O dependencies.
context: Defines the core domain models for skills: `Skill` and `SkillTemplate`. These are pure domain types with no I/O dependencies.

rules:
`Skill` MUST be an interface with:

- `name: string` — unique identifier for the skill
- `description: string` — human-readable description of what the skill does
- `templates: SkillTemplate[]` — template files associated with this skill

`SkillTemplate` MUST be an interface with:

- `filename: string` — template file name
- `getContent(): Promise<string>` — lazy content loading, returns the template content
The domain layer MUST NOT contain any I/O (file system, network, etc.). All I/O happens in the infrastructure layer.
Template content MUST be loaded lazily via `getContent()`. The `Skill` object itself MUST NOT hold loaded content in memory.

constraints: Domain types MUST be pure TypeScript interfaces.
No dependencies on node:fs or any I/O library.

scenarios.name: Skill creation
then: it has name, description, and templates properties

scenarios.name: Lazy content loading
when: getContent() is called on a SkillTemplate
then: it returns the template content as a Promise

scenarios.name: Domain has no I/O imports
then: no imports from node:fs or similar I/O modules exist
